executive_summary: The Iran-US armed conflict has escalated dramatically with confirmation of multiple major developments: IRGC claims assassination attempt on Israeli PM Netanyahu and IAF Commander; US Central Command confirms 3 F-15E jets shot down (reportedly by Kuwaiti air defense in friendly fire); Iranian strikes hit the Natanz nuclear facility; Iranian death toll reaches 555; and Gulf states jointly condemn Iranian attacks. The conflict now spans eight fronts with direct US-Iran military engagement confirmed. Internal chaos reported in Tehran leadership with repeated decision reversals in under 24h.
whats_new: IRGC claims assassination attempt on Israeli PM Netanyahu and IAF Commander Bar in Tel Aviv — marking direct escalation against Israeli leadership
US Central Command confirms 3 F-15E Strike Eagles shot down over Kuwait — officially stated as friendly fire by Kuwaiti air defense during Iranian attack; all 6 aircrew recovered
Natanz nuclear facility confirmed struck by US-Israeli strikes — Iran reports attack on 'peaceful, safeguarded' nuclear site; IAEA notified
Kuwait Ministry of Defense officially announces downing American warplanes — marks direct escalation with Kuwait becoming theater of hostilities

Internal leadership chaos in Tehran: Reports indicate intense disagreement within surviving Iranian leadership with repeated critical decision reversals in under 24h, potentially impairing war coordination
confirmed_events: Multi-source confirmed: 3 US F-15E jets shot down over Kuwait by Kuwaiti air defenses (friendly fire) — CENTCOM and Kuwaiti MOD both confirm; 6 aircrew safely ejected
Multi-source confirmed: Iranian strikes on UAE killed 3 civilians, injured 58 — 165 ballistic missiles and suicide drones launched at Emirati territory
Multi-source confirmed: Israeli airstrikes on Beirut southern suburbs killed 31, wounded 149 — ongoing operations against Hezbollah
Multi-source confirmed: 555 civilians killed in Iran from US-Israeli strikes — Iranian Red Crescent reports; 10 medical facilities now targeted
Multi-source confirmed: Gulf states (Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, UAE) jointly condemn Iranian missile/drone attacks — coordinated diplomatic response
unverified_emerging: IRGC assassination attempt on Israeli PM Netanyahu — IRGC claims responsibility but no independent Israeli confirmation of targeting at that level; single-source claim from Iranian media
Hezbollah targeting Haifa — claim from Sahafi Jordan citing Hezbollah communique, but not independently verified; follows group's formal entry into war
Iranian hypersonic Fattah-2 missiles deployed — TASS reports first combat use, but tactical impact not independently corroborated
Putin discussing Middle East escalation with UAE President — reported by RIA Novosti, diplomatic engagement but no details on Russian position
US forces at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar targeted — Qatar condemns Iranian attack on territory but extent of US base damage unconfirmed

people_travel: UK Foreign Office compiling register of 300,000 British nationals in Gulf region for safety guidance; all flights out cancelled
Iraq extends airspace closure for 48 hours until Wednesday — all incoming, outgoing, transit flights suspended
Cyprus: Drone strike on British Akrotiri airbase confirmed by Cypriot President — minor damage reported
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz crisis escalating — roughly 20% of world oil passes through; Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping compound disruptions
Persian Gulf shipping complications intensifying — expert assessment cites significant implications for commercial routes and oil transport
Global supply chain vulnerability heightened — conflict disrupting trade through critical maritime chokepoints
market_macro: Oil prices surged following Iran strikes — global markets react to spreading Middle East conflict
Stock markets decline — investors flee risk assets amid regional escalation
Commodity price volatility expected — energy market uncertainty impacting inflation and interest rate projections

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict will continue escalating over 24-72h. Iran has demonstrated ability to sustain missile/drone campaigns across multiple fronts (Gulf states, Israel, Iraq, Syria) and will likely continue retaliatory strikes. Internal Iranian leadership chaos may impair coordination but also creates unpredictable decision-making. US and Israeli forces will continue operations against Iranian infrastructure (nuclear, military, proxies). Hezbollah formal entry into war expands Lebanon front significantly. Gulf states increasingly aligned with US position following joint condemnation of Iranian attacks.
escalation_risks: Further Iranian attacks on Gulf state territory — UAE, Qatar, Saudi Arabia already targeted; Bahrain likely next
Nuclear facility escalation — Natanz hit; Bushehr and other sites at risk; could trigger broader WMD concerns
Direct Iran-US naval confrontation in Gulf — US 5th Fleet operations, Strait of Hormuz tensions
Houthi formal entry into conflict — currently threatened, would open new Red Sea front
Israeli-Hezbollah Lebanon escalation — 31 killed in Beirut strikes; ground operation possible
Russian/Chinese intervention — Putin discussing with UAE; potential for external powers to back Iran
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No clear de-escalation pathway visible. Iran has rejected all diplomatic engagement (confirmed in previous briefing). Trump statement suggests US believes Iran 'should have made deal a week ago' but military operations continue at intensity. Internal leadership chaos in Tehran may eventually force pragmatic decisions, but current trajectory points to further escalation. Gulf state mediation possible but Qatar/UAE already condemned Iranian attacks. Russian diplomacy marginal so far. Ceasefire unlikely without major concession from one side that neither appears willing to make.
